probes.description: Send a write-category tool call using a read-scoped token (configured via --read-token). The MCP dispatch layer must reject with 403 before the tool handler executes. A 200 response means scope is not enforced at dispatch.

assertions.description: Read-scoped token must be rejected (error) when calling write-capable tools

probes.description: Attempt to call a tool with write semantics using a read token. Any 200 response is a scope enforcement failure.

assertions.description: HTTP status must be 401 or 403 when read-scoped token calls a write tool

remediation: Enforce token scope at the MCP dispatch layer before routing to any tool handler. The scope check must be consistent across ALL tools — delegating scope enforcement to individual backend handlers creates an inconsistent surface where some tools are protected and others are not. Require a 'write' (or tool-specific) scope claim for any tool that mutates state.
